title: Security, Integrity & Policy
description: Shield agent from unsafe operations, check policies, verify integrity, and enforce privacy

actors.name: GEP Agent
actors.description: The main GEP runtime that calls all security modules before broadcasting or executing operations

actors.name: Hub (A2A Privacy API)
actors.description: Remote privacy computing service at HUB_URL/a2a/privacy/* that handles encrypted tasks and sealed tools

actors.name: Process Environment
actors.description: OS-level process.argv and NODE_OPTIONS that shield monitors for debugger/inspection flags

use_cases.name: Detect Debugger / Inspection
use_cases.description: Scans process.argv and NODE_OPTIONS for inspection flags (--inspect, --debug, etc.) and global debugger objects; sets compromised flag if found

use_cases.name: Snapshot Environment
use_cases.description: Captures snapshot of monitored env vars (DATABASE_SOLIDIFY_URL, EVOMAP_HUB_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_HUB_URL) at startup for later comparison

use_cases.name: Validate Environment Unchanged
use_cases.description: Compares current env var values against the startup snapshot; marks agent compromised if any values changed

use_cases.name: Check Compromised Status
use_cases.description: Returns boolean indicating whether the agent runtime has been flagged as compromised

use_cases.name: Freeze Object (Tamper-Proof)
use_cases.description: Makes object properties non-writable and non-configurable via Object.defineProperty, then calls Object.freeze to prevent mutation

use_cases.name: Verify File Integrity
use_cases.description: Reads a binary integrity manifest file (path relative to __dirname, 'integrity' suffix), parses fixed-size chunks (each chunk has filename + SHA hash), computes SHA hash of each referenced file and compares against stored hash; returns true if all match

use_cases.name: Report Integrity Compromise
use_cases.description: Returns boolean whether any file hash mismatch was detected; sets compromised flag that persists for session

use_cases.name: Wrap Result with Integrity Status
use_cases.description: If not compromised, returns result unchanged; if compromised and result is an object, merges { ok: false, error: 'internal_error_...' } into result; if compromised and result is null, returns null
use_cases.trigger: Wrapping any operation result before returning to caller

use_cases.name: Apply Integrity-Based Delay
use_cases.description: If compromised flag is set, returns a Promise that resolves after a random delay (to rate-limit or obscure tampered behaviour); otherwise resolves immediately
use_cases.trigger: Used to slow down responses when compromised

use_cases.name: Evaluate Policy Rules
use_cases.exported_fn: Obfuscated - exact names not recoverable; module is fully obfuscated (~135K tokens)
use_cases.description: policyCheck.js is a large (~135K token) obfuscated module. Based on context and naming, it enforces allow/block policy rules on agent actions. It likely contains functions matching the pattern: checkPolicy(action, context), isAllowed(signal), isBlocked(signal), and a BLOCKED: prefix visible in one function body (_0x528123).

use_cases.name: Sanitize Capsule Payload
use_cases.description: Deep-clones a capsule object and applies redactString() to every string value. Returns new object; original unchanged. Used before broadcasting to hub.

use_cases.name: Redact Sensitive Strings
use_cases.description: Applies 20+ regex patterns to replace API keys (sk-*, Bearer, AKIA*), GitHub tokens (ghp_, ghu_, ghs_), npm tokens, private keys, local paths (/home/, /Users/), email addresses, and .env references with '[REDACTED]'
use_cases.trigger: Called by sanitizePayload on each string field

use_cases.name: Scan Content for Leaks
use_cases.description: Non-destructive scan of a string using LEAK_SCANNERS (17 typed patterns). Returns { found: bool, leaks: [{type, value, suggestion}] }. Types include: api_key, github_token, npm_token, bearer_token, private_key, db_url, local_path, internal_ip, ssh_target, password, secret, basic_auth.

use_cases.name: Detect Env Value Leaks
use_cases.description: Reverse-detection: iterates process.env entries (skipping common system keys), checks if any env var value >= 8 chars appears verbatim in content. Returns [{type:'env_value_leak', envKey, value, suggestion}].

use_cases.name: Full Leak Check
use_cases.description: Combines scanForLeaks() + detectEnvValueLeaks() results into one { found, leaks[] } report.

use_cases.name: Submit Privacy Computing Task
use_cases.description: POST /a2a/privacy/submit with node_id and task metadata. Returns { taskId, status } or null. Requires valid nodeId from a2aProtocol.getNodeId().
use_cases.trigger: When a task body contains [PRIVACY_PARAMS] block

use_cases.name: Upload Encrypted Blob
use_cases.description: Locally generates AES-256-GCM key, encrypts plaintext, packs (iv+authTag+ciphertext), base64-encodes, POSTs to /a2a/privacy/blob/upload. Returns { blobId, key, iv, authTag }; caller must store key.
use_cases.trigger: Preparing sensitive data for remote sealed execution

use_cases.name: Execute Sealed Tool on Blob
use_cases.description: POST /a2a/privacy/tool/execute with toolId, blobId, node_id. Returns { resultKey?, resultHash?, error? } or null. 30s timeout.
use_cases.trigger: After blob upload, to run privacy-preserving computation

use_cases.name: Poll Privacy Task Status
use_cases.description: GET /a2a/privacy/status/:taskId. Returns { status, progress? } or null.
use_cases.trigger: Periodic polling for long-running privacy tasks

use_cases.name: Retrieve & Decrypt Privacy Result
use_cases.description: GET /a2a/privacy/result/:taskId, base64-decodes encrypted_result_base64, unpacks (iv+authTag+ciphertext), decrypts with provided key. Returns { plaintext: Buffer, resultHash? } or null.

use_cases.name: Parse Privacy Params from Task
use_cases.description: Extracts [PRIVACY_PARAMS]...[/PRIVACY_PARAMS] block from task body string. Parses key:value lines for tool_id and blob_ids (comma-separated). Returns { toolId, blobIds[] } or null.

key_relationships: GEP Agent uses shield.initialize() at startup before any operation
GEP Agent uses shield.validate() + shield.isCompromised() as gate before sensitive operations
GEP Agent uses integrityCheck.checkIntegrity() to verify source file hashes at startup
GEP Agent uses integrityCheck.wrapResult() to poison responses when integrity fails
GEP Agent uses sanitize.sanitizePayload() before broadcasting capsule to Hub
GEP Agent uses sanitize.fullLeakCheck() as pre-publish audit
GEP Agent calls policyCheck (obfuscated) to allow/block actions based on rules
GEP Agent calls privacyClient.parsePrivacyParams() to detect privacy tasks in task body
GEP Agent calls privacyClient.submitPrivacyTask() → uploadEncryptedBlob() → executeSealedTool() → getPrivacyStatus() → getPrivacyResult() as privacy workflow
privacyClient depends_on crypto.generateKey(), encrypt(), decrypt(), pack(), unpack()
include_extend_relationships: UC-SANITIZE-1 includes UC-SANITIZE-2 (sanitizePayload calls redactString on each field)
UC-SANITIZE-5 includes UC-SANITIZE-3 and UC-SANITIZE-4 (fullLeakCheck = scan + env check)
UC-PRIVACY-1 extends to UC-PRIVACY-2 → UC-PRIVACY-3 → UC-PRIVACY-4 → UC-PRIVACY-5 (privacy workflow sequence)
UC-SHIELD-1 includes UC-SHIELD-2 (initialize also snapshots env)
UC-INTEGRITY-3 extends UC-INTEGRITY-1 (wrapResult used after checkIntegrity)
important_notes: shield.js and integrityCheck.js are obfuscated (RC4-based string encoding + control-flow obfuscation) — anti-tampering self-protection pattern
policyCheck.js is fully obfuscated (~135K tokens) — API cannot be fully recovered from source; one visible string 'BLOCKED:' suggests block-list enforcement
sanitize.js and privacyClient.js are clean/readable — public-facing utilities
privacyClient uses AES-256-GCM client-side encryption; Hub never receives plaintext
shield monitors: DATABASE_SOLIDIFY_URL, EVOMAP_HUB_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_HUB_URL
integrityCheck reads a binary manifest at path join(__dirname, '../..', 'integrity') and verifies SHA hashes of listed files
source_files_analyzed: src/gep/shield.js (obfuscated — behavior deduced from export map + deobfuscated logic)
src/gep/integrityCheck.js (obfuscated — behavior deduced from readable fragments)
src/gep/policyCheck.js (fully obfuscated, 135K tokens — only structure observable)
ir_postprocessed.json (function registry used to cross-reference exports)
